title: DETERMINATION OF SALINITY TOLERANCE IN TOMATO CULTIVARS GROWN IN BASRAH, IRAQ
author: Abdul Baset Muhammad Mahdi Almathi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The development of salt-tolerant tomato genotypes is a basic necessity to defeat the challenges of tomato production under salinity in the field or soil-free farming. Two types of salinity tolerance genes were used SOD and GPX using real time PCR technique under different concentration of  NaCl (3, 6 and 9 decimals ), for different groups of tomato plants.
steps_raw: The development of salt-tolerant tomato genotypes is a basic necessity to defeat the challenges of tomato production under salinity in the field or soil-free farming. Two types of salinity tolerance genes were used SOD and GPX using real time PCR technique under different concentration of  NaCl (3, 6 and 9 decimals ), for different groups of tomato plants.
keywords: Solanum Spp.; SOD; GPX ; tomato; salt stress; salt-stress related genes.
